user.title: Weather Network Uploader
user.description: Choose which weather networks to publish to and how often. Each network is rate-limited separately, so you can pick a fast polling interval without overloading slower services.

data_description.interval: How often to read your sensors, in seconds. Each network is throttled separately to respect its own rate limit, so a fast value here will not overload slower services. 60 matches RMI's recommendation for WOW-BE.

credentials.title: Credentials for {service}
credentials.description: Enter the station identifier and authentication key issued by {service}. These are stored in cleartext in Home Assistant's .storage directory and are transmitted to the provider over TLS.

data_description.station_id: For WOW-BE this is the site ID from your registration email; the short form or the long UUID both work. For CWOP it is your callsign, e.g. EW9876. For Windy it is the station number.
data_description.key: The authentication key issued or chosen at registration. Any value is accepted; no length or format is enforced.
data_description.latitude: Decimal degrees. Round to ~3 decimals (about 100 m) unless you want your exact location published.
data_description.longitude: Decimal degrees. Round to ~3 decimals (about 100 m) unless you want your exact location published.
data_description.altitude: Station ground elevation in metres above sea level.

sensors.title: Map your sensors
sensors.description: Select the Home Assistant entity for each field you want to publish. Leave a field blank to omit it. Units are converted automatically from each entity's declared unit. Note: rain_rate must be an instantaneous rate sensor, not an hourly accumulation. Readings that fall outside a sane range for a field (for example a pressure that looks like Pascals rather than hPa) are dropped at runtime and flagged on the 'Source data problem' sensor.

confirm_mapping.title: Check sensor mapping

owm_station.title: OpenWeatherMap setup
owm_station.description:
OpenWeatherMap stations are created through its API — there is no website signup, and the station ID you need for uploads only exists once a station has been created.

Enter your OpenWeatherMap API key, then choose whether to create a new station now or use the internal ID of one you already have.

data_description.key: Your OpenWeatherMap API key (from the API keys tab of your account).
data_description.mode: Create a new station, or enter an existing internal station ID.

owm_create.title: Create OpenWeatherMap station
owm_create.description: This registers a new station on your OpenWeatherMap account and stores the internal ID it returns. The location is pre-filled from your Home Assistant settings — adjust it if you'd rather publish a rounded or different location. If a station with the same identifier already exists, it is reused rather than duplicated.

data_description.external_id: A short label of your choosing (letters, digits, underscores). Not the internal ID — OpenWeatherMap generates that.
data_description.name: Pre-filled from your Home Assistant location name. A display name for the station on OpenWeatherMap.
data_description.latitude: Pre-filled from Home Assistant. Round to ~3 decimals (about 100 m) if you don't want your exact location published.
data_description.longitude: Pre-filled from Home Assistant. Round to ~3 decimals (about 100 m) if you don't want your exact location published.
data_description.altitude: Pre-filled from your Home Assistant elevation. Station ground elevation in metres.

owm_existing.title: Existing OpenWeatherMap station
owm_existing.description: Enter the internal station ID (a long hexadecimal string like 583436dd9643a9000196b8d6). You can find it in the API response when the station was created, or by listing your stations via the API.

data_description.station_id: The internal ID OpenWeatherMap generated for the station — not the label you chose.

error.invalid_auth: OpenWeatherMap rejected the API key. Check it and try again.
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach OpenWeatherMap. Check your connection and try again.
error.cannot_create: OpenWeatherMap could not create the station. Its response is in the log.

confirm_mapping.title: Check sensor mapping

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Adjust the polling interval, sensor staleness limit, and sensor mapping. To change a network's credentials, remove and re-add it.

data_description.interval: How often to read your sensors, in seconds. Each network is throttled separately to respect its own rate limit.
data_description.max_sensor_age: Drop readings whose entity has not reported for this long. This tracks whether your station is still sending data, not whether the value is changing, so a rain sensor reading 0.0 through a dry week is unaffected. Set 0 to disable (not recommended: a station that dies would then have its last reading republished as current, forever).

add_network.title: Add a weather network
add_network.description: Choose one or more networks to add. You'll enter credentials for each on the next screens.

remove_network.title: Remove a weather network
remove_network.description: Choose one or more networks to remove. Their stored credentials are deleted and uploads to them stop. Your sensor mapping is not affected.

abort.all_networks_configured: Every supported network is already configured.
abort.no_networks_configured: There are no networks to remove.

options.cwop: CWOP / NOAA (feeds MADIS + National Weather Service)
